title: MIBI: MIBI staining of fresh-frozen/OCT-embedded samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cnhkl","text":"This protocol entails the recommended staining procedure for Multiplex Ion Beam Imaging Time of Flight instrument (MIBI_TOF) as developed in the Sean C. Bendall and Michael R. Angelo labs, and has been adapted in the lab of Felix Hartmann specifically for the staining of fresh-frozen samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fqo1m","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"d29pp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"63vau","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"citation","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"align":"","authors":"Marc Bosse, Sean Bendall, Mike Angelo","can_edit":true,"date":"","doi":"https://protocols.io/view/mibi-staining-b9b3r2qn","guid":"26D67740F49411EDB3327911D9A434A9","id":0,"journal":"protocols.io","protocol_id":62555,"title":"MIBI staining"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"91l3v","text":"Staining tissue sections is fairly straightforward but there are few things to be cognizant of, when preparing samples:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c5o0j","text":"Always try to limit mechanical damage to the sample surface. This can occur when moving the samples with forceps, particularly when the mounting substrates are small.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"og6k","text":"Once the samples have been rehydrated, they cannot dry out until the end of the protocol.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3u92n","text":"Be careful at all times to not touch the tissue, in order to not leave any residue.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"10s52","text":"Once the samples have been stained, fixed, and dehydrated, they have an indefinite shelf life and can be imaged at any time but need to be stored properly such as in a vacuum chamber or in a sealed vacuum bag","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bah89","text":"MIBI_TOF observes the basic principles of Mass spectrometry. Any contaminant ions present in water or in the air can potentially compromise the integrity of the sample.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bg73g","text":"Therefore to prevent potential contamination, it is important to always use single use lab ware containers to make the solutions. The protocol has been validated using the level of precision of graduated Nalgene bottles and 50 mL tubes. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":65,"length":41}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3olnf","text":"The use of washed beakers or graduated cylinders is not considered as good a practice, due possible introduction of contaminants (exemples of sources: barium from lab ware soap or calcium from air dried lab ware)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":212}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7g3lq","text":"Verify the stocks of all reagents and place an order or prepare solutions, if some reagents are running low.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"325ka","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"5bnbl","text":"All organic solvents should be manipulated under a chemical hood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Verify stock of 1x PBS wash buffer and prepare accordingly if running low This buffer does NOT contain Tween! A B 1 Reagents Quantity of 250mL 2 PBS 10x (ml) 25mL 3 Bovine Albumin (BSA), heat shock treated (g) 0.25g 4 Ultrapure (type 1) water (mL) 225mL Constituents of PBS washing buffer
Transfer the slides in the first MIBI 1x PBS wash buffer and dip shortly to remove PFA
Transfer the slides to the second 1x PBS wash buffer and dip shortly to remove PFA
Add 200µL of blocking buffer to the sequenza assembly, make sure the buffer is retained in the assembly (the waterline should stop at the upper end of the capillary space)
Incubate at Room temperature for 01:00:00

Prepare antibody mix based on the putative multiplex antibody panel Make sure that all the antibodies are ready to use BEFORE starting to build the panel It is highly recommended to prepare all the antibodies, ready to use, a day before the panel is built
The total volume needed for staining each Sequenza slide assembly is 120 µL The manufacturer recommend to use 100 µL per Sequenza slide assembly. 120 µL is therefore a 20% excess.

Build an antibody mix table information to make the antibody panel as follow: Conjugation ID, Target name, Channel, Antibody concentration, Titer, Volume Exemple: The total volume needed for staining each Sequenza slide assembly is 120 µL A B C D E F 1 ID Target Channel Concentration µg/mL Titer (µg/mL) Volume (µL) 2 1565 CD45 169 50 0.25 0.75 3 1516 CD8 158 50 0.5 1.5 4 ... ... ... ... ... ... 5 Total 150 6 Antibody mix 2.25 7 Blocking buffer 147.75 For Blocking Buffer solution preparation refer to step 4
Pre-wet spin column Add 400 µL of clean blocking buffer to a Centrifugal 0.1 µm filter unit (Millipore, UFC30VV00)
10000 rcf, Room temperature, 1 minute , discard flowthrough
Add antibody mix to the filter unit
10000 rcf, Room temperature, 1 minute
Add filtered antibody master mix (120µL) onto the sequenza assembly
Place the moist chamber at 4˚C Overnight , preferably in a place with low disturbance
Following overnight incubation, wash twice with 1 mL of wash buffer
After the 1h incubation, wash twice with PBS wash buffer within the sequenza assembly by pipetting 2x 1mL.

Prepare fresh 2% glutaraldehyde fixing solution Glutaraldehyde fixing solution Add 30 mL of 1x PBS low barium in a 50 mL tube Break the glass glutaraldehyde 8% (amber vial) Add the glutaraldehyde (10 mL) to the diluent by inverting it and tapping the bottom of the vial Transfer the content in a linear stainer container

Set the linear stainer containers Fill containers with the following solution and order Glutaraldehyde x 1, PBS low barium x 1, TRIS 100 mm pH 8.5 x 3, ddH2O x 2, 70% Ethanol x1, 80% Ethanol x1, 95% Ethanol x 2, 100% Ethanol x 2, exit stainless steel tank = empty
Mount the slides on the linear slide holder Fix in 2% glutaraldehyde for 00:05:00
Rinse briefly with 1x PBS low barium

Press on Menu Check for Processing time = 30 sec, Lift bar = 976, Number of dips = 3 Continue to press Menu until the screen displays Start at: __ Set Start position corresponding to the first slide carrier position Exemple: If the first slide carrier is at position 3, use Plus ( + ) or Minus ( - ) button to increase or decrease to get Start at: 03
Then press Enter
Press Run on the Linear Stainer
Allow the dehydration process and wait until the slides reached the empty stainless steel tank and stop
Store the slides immediately under vacuum until MIBI acquisition Alternatively, the stained slides can be stored in a vacuum sealed bag for longterm storage pre and post MIBI acquisition
If using frozen and vacuumed slides (e.g. from collaborators), let slides come to room temperature before destroying the vacuum seal ! (prevent condensation!) ~5-10min

Prepare Blocking buffer (also used as antibody diluent in this protocol!) This buffer does NOT contain Tween! A B 1 Reagents Qty for 5mL 2 PBS 1x 4750µL 3 Horse Serum 250µL Constituents of blocking buffer Filter with .45µm syringe
Prepare 1x PBS by diluting 20xPBS 1:20 in Ultrapure type 1 water (needed for blocking buffer)
Fix Slides after thawing in 10% Neutral Buffered Formaline (NBF) for 1h at room temperature

After washing, transfer slide into antigen retrieval buffer jar within the preheated PT module press run on the digital screen as soon as the device is preheated to 75°C. The display will show "WARMUP" and heat up to 80°C for retrieval , stay at this temperature for 20minutes, direcly put tissue back to RT. This process takes ~30min.
In the morning: heat up PT Module and prepare fresh antigen retrieval solution (step 6) to place in the device for preheating

To prepare fresh antigen retrieval solution , dilute 10x DAKO (3-in-1) pH9 antigen retrieval solution 1:10 in ultrapure (type 1) water (e.g. for 25mL: 2.5mL + 22.5mL) note: if retrieving in gold slide delivery chamber (5 slots), 25mL are sufficient A B C 1 Total volume (mL) Volume target retrieval (mL) Volume (mL) ddH2O 2 25 2.5 22.5 3 50 5 45 4 100 10 90 Dilution table for Dako Antigen retrieval solution
Take slides out of the PT module and let them cool to room temperature (~10 min) before proceeding.
Fill a disposable Pipetting Reservoir with 20 mL of 1x PBS wash buffer
Place sample slide on a Sequenza cover plate aligning the bottom slide with the notches on the cover plate (see picture below panel A)

Fill by capillarity the space between the slide and cover plate by holding the parts tight and dipping the bottom part of the assembly in the wash buffer reservoir (see picture above panel B). If capillary force is not sufficient to fill the staining reservoir, a careful pumping motion on the triangular protrusion can help.
Transfer the slide and coverplate assembly into the Sequenza rack. Slip in the assembly (see below picture panel C)
Secure the assembly and make sure that the assembly placed down in the rack (see above picture panel D)
Add 1 mL of wash buffer. The buffer should flow thru within 1 min 30 s. Repeat by adding 1 mL of wash buffer

Prepare the antibody mix according to the calculation. Make sure to spin down antibody vials before pipetting to prevent pipetting of aggregates. Use filter tips when pipetting from communal stocks and keep the tubes on ice.
Disassemble the sequenza setup. Make sure at this point that the slide doesn't dry out and directly proceed to the next steps.
If embedding medium is still on slide, carefully dip slide into 1x PBS before fixation
creator: Sven Truxa
